If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.58-9 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** No **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/86714 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** osidnjosjdisnjodnsjio@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Mac 26.2 / Chrome **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Create a new, unvalidated Gmail account. 3. Open 1:1 DM with any user. 4. Click + > Pay. 5. Enter amount > Next > Mark as paid. 6. Open FAB. 7. Click Pay under Quick action. 8. Enter amount and select USD. 9. Click Pay > Pay with personal account. ## Expected Result: Magic code page will open because the account is not validated yet. ## Actual Result: Nothing happens after clicking Pay with personal account when paying from QAB. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6b29350d-28f4-4c58-9c83-99b06a9a9546 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.58-7 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @joekaufmanexpensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1776085493693459) ## Action Performed: 1. Create a report and add several non-reimbursable expenses to it. 2. Submit and approve the report. 3. Note the pay button appears on the report, but we don't add the report to the pay queue on reports > to-do > pay. 4. Go to Inbox. ## Expected Result: We don't show the pinned pay action in the LHN for reports with only non-reimbursable expenses. Paying these reports is optional, and many customers won't do it. Pinning them will clog the LHN for these customers with actions that will never be resolved, making it less usable. ## Actual Result: We show the pinned pay action in the LHN for reports with only non-reimbursable reports. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea530148-14fc-4b3a-a3c6-f9f286cac921 https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed02877e-1f87-4a3f-af99-cc0069702de0 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.57-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @PedroEspinoza1999 **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1775836082832439) ## Action Performed: 1. Log into New Expensify as an admin. 2. Attempt to open a specific expense report. 3. Observe the error message. 4. Log into Expensify Classic. 5. Navigate to the same report. 6. Confirm that the report is visible and accessible in Classic. ## Expected Result: The admin should be able to access the same report in both Classic and New Expensify. Report visibility and permissions should be consistent across platforms. ## Actual Result: In New Expensify, the admin receives the error: “Hmm... it's not here. This chat or expense may have been deleted or you do not have access to it.” In Classic, the report is accessible without issues. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshot for the issue is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1775836082832439) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Lazy-Load RightModalNavigator

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/77171#issuecomment-4215299136 ### Problem When the web app starts, non-critical blocking work on the startup critical path delays the splash screen from hiding, which prevents users from seeing and interacting with the app. ### Solution Lazy-load RightModalNavigator in src/libs/Navigation/AppNavigator/AuthScreens.tsx: Change RightModalNavigator from component={RightModalNavigator} (eager, static import) to getComponent={loadRightModalNavigator} (lazy, deferred require). This follows the exact same pattern already used for 12 other screens in the same file (e.g., loadHomePage, loadReportSplitNavigator, loadWorkspaceNavigator). The RightModalNavigator and its 48 sub-navigators with 702 screen references are deferred from the startup chunk to first RHP navigation. Since no RHP screen is visible at startup, this work is entirely unnecessary on the critical path.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @sobitneupane

title: [$250] Broken card connection violation message not shown to workspace admins

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven't already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! **Connection with other issues:** Split from Expensify/App#72055 --- ## Action Performed: 1. Create a workspace with a company card feed connected 2. As a workspace admin (who is **not** the expense submitter), view an expense that has a `brokenCardConnection` violation (i.e., the bank connection for the company card is broken) 3. Observe the violation message displayed on the expense ## Expected Result: The workspace admin should see the message: **"Receipt pending due to broken bank connection. Please resolve in Company cards"** — with a link to the Company cards settings page. This is the behavior implemented in [`BrokenConnectionDescription`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/BrokenConnectionDescription.tsx#L42-L44) for the `isPolicyAdmin && !isCurrentUserSubmitter(report)` code path. ## Actual Result: The admin only sees a generic "Review" message. The `brokenCardConnection`-specific violation message does not appear. ## Root Cause There is a conflict between two layers of violation filtering logic: 1. [`shouldShowViolation`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/TransactionUtils/index.ts#L1713-L1715) gates **all** `rter` violations (including `brokenCardConnection` type) on `(isSubmitter || isInstantSubmitEnabled(policy))`. For a workspace admin who is **not** the expense submitter, on a policy **without** instant submit, the RTER violation is filtered out entirely. 2. [`BrokenConnectionDescription`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/components/BrokenConnectionDescription.tsx#L42-L44) has a code path specifically for `isPolicyAdmin && !isCurrentUserSubmitter(report)` that renders the admin message — but this is **dead code** because the violation was already removed upstream by `shouldShowViolation`. 3. [`shouldShowBrokenConnectionViolationInternal`](https://github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/src/libs/TransactionUtils/index.ts#L1608-L1622) also has logic to show the violation to admins for open reports, but its input is already empty because `shouldShowViolation` filtered the violations first. The filtering chain is: `useTransactionViolations` → calls `shouldShowViolation` → removes the RTER violation → `shouldShowBrokenConnectionViolation` receives empty array → `BrokenConnectionDescription` never renders. ## Workaround None known. ## Platform: All platforms. ## Version Number: Main (current) ## Reproducible in staging?: Yes ## Reproducible in production?: Yes ## Logs: N/A ## Expensify/Expensify Issue URL: N/A ## Notes/Photos/Videos: N/A --- **Originated from:** Expensify/App#72055 — [MelvinBot's analysis comment](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/72055#issuecomment-4129833145)

Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @thelullabyy
